issue_title: proposal: dropping develop branch and use only master
issue_body:
I think that in the last years the use of two branches, master and develop proved to be more of a confusion factor and caused more maintenance work.

I would like to propose dropping use of develop branch and using directly master.

Please use github up/down voting buttons on this as I don't want to make a decision without getting enough feedback from a signifiant number of contributors (probably 5 at least).

issue_title: Hardcoding of file path of "sd-messages.h" preventing using this in build systems
issue_body:
Hi,

We are trying to build python-systemd for a different architecture using yocto build system, but due to the hardcoding of the file path of ```sd-messages.h``` in ```setup.py``` we are unable to do so unless the systemd header files are installed on the build system and are of the same version as that of the target build

Offending lines:
~~~~
constants = [line.split()[1]
                         for line in open("/usr/include/systemd/sd-messages.h")
                         if line.startswith('#define SD_MESSAGE_')]
~~~~

The code should instead take a relative path to systemd headers and use that instead of the absolute path. Will send you out a pull request for the same if it is acceptable or please suggest an alternative.

issue_body:
Below, is the current output of `manticore -h`. It is overwhelming, especially to new users, due to all of the optional arguments. Most of the arguments, are legacy, poorly documented, rarely used, and simply create noise.

Ideally, we should remove the ones that are truly unnecessary, but for the meantime, I propose using `help=argparse.SUPPRESS` to hide them from the help output. This will make Manticore more approachable while maintaining backwards compatibility.

Specifically, I propose hiding:
- assertions
- buffer
- context
- names
- offset
- replay - this one is totally unused, we should remove it.

```
usage: manticore [-h] [--assertions ASSERTIONS] [--buffer BUFFER]
                 [--context CONTEXT] [--coverage COVERAGE] [--data DATA]
                 [--env ENV] [--file FILES] [--names NAMES] [--offset OFFSET]
                 [--policy POLICY] [--profile] [--procs PROCS]
                 [--replay REPLAY] [--size SIZE] [--timeout TIMEOUT] [-v]
                 [--workspace WORKSPACE] [--version]
                 argv [argv ...]

Symbolically analyze a program

positional arguments:
  argv                  Path to program, and arguments ('+' in arguments
                        indicates symbolic byte).

optional arguments:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit
  --assertions ASSERTIONS
                        File with additional assertions
  --buffer BUFFER       Specify buffer to make symbolic
  --context CONTEXT     path to file with additional context
  --coverage COVERAGE   where to write the coverage data
  --data DATA           Initial concrete concrete_data for the input symbolic
                        buffer
  --env ENV             Specify symbolic environment variable VARNAME=++++++
  --file FILES          Specify symbolic input file, '+' marks symbolic bytes
  --names NAMES         File with function addresses to replace with known
                        models
  --offset OFFSET       Buffer header size to leave concrete
  --policy POLICY       Search policy.
                        random|adhoc|uncovered|dicount|icount|syscount|depth.
                        (use + (max) or - (min) to specify order. e.g.
                        +random)
  --profile             Enable profiling mode.
  --procs PROCS         Number of parallel processes to spawn
  --replay REPLAY       The trace filename to replay
  --size SIZE           Specify buffer full size
  --timeout TIMEOUT     Timeout. Abort exploration aftr TIMEOUT seconds
  -v                    Specify verbosity level from -v to -vvvv
  --workspace WORKSPACE
                        A folder name for temporaries and results.(default
                        mcore_?????)
  --version             Show program version information

```

issue_title: Options for configuring 'LogSink.destination'.
issue_body:
When I drafted the [logging usage doc](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/gcloud-python/logging-api/docs/logging-usage.rst), I assumed that users would want to pass in a string identifying the bucket, bigquery dataset, or pubsub topic, to which log entries would be exported, and I guessed at three properties, `storage_bucket`, `bigquery_dataset`, and `pubsub_topic`, which would consume and validate those strings.

Now, while working on the implementation, I believe that it might be better to have a `destination` property, whose setter would be willing to take any of:

- a string (it would do no validation, but just store it).
- a `Bucket` instance
- a `Dataset` instance
- a `Topic` instance.

In the case of the last three, the `destination` property setter would synthesize the appropriate destination string and store it.

We would then be able to accept a `destination` argument to `Sink.__init__` and `Client.sink`, which would just rely on the `destination` property setter.

Thoughts?

issue_title: Snapshot manipulation Python API
issue_body:
Python code needs the ability to manipulate Snapshots in order to pass them to (or interpret them from) things like process execution.

At the very least, we're going to need to be able to compose snapshots (`union(toolchain_snapshot, sources_snapshot)`).

We need to work out how we'd like to phrase this on the Python side. Two ends of the spectrum:
1. Give the whole protobuf messages to the Python, allow the Python to inspect/merge the protos, and hand new ones to the Rust code.
2. The rust code exposes intrinsics along the lines of `Snapshots.merge(snapshot1, snapshot2)`.

This will probably also shape how we hand snapshots across the boundary. Right now, we're passing a serialization of a `Digest` (a fingerprint + proto-bytes-length). That may change.

issue_title: antlr3 generated target has unstable hash and uncache-able due to timestamp in generated code
issue_body:
antlr3 puts a comment with timestamp at the top of generated java sources, causing the target artifact to have an ever-changing fp which becomes un-cacheable. Any targets dependent on an antlr target will also be uncacheable due to ever-changing dependent hashes, causing build slowdown. Proposed fix is either

1. scrub the generated sources to remove timestamps
2. change fingerprint strategy for java_antlr_library so that it hashes the original .g source rather than the generated

issue_title: Tune the polling frequency of remote execution GetOperation calls
issue_body: Right now, we tight-loop calling GetOperation in https://github.com/pantsbuild/pants/blob/master/src/rust/engine/process_execution/src/remote.rs - we should probably use some kind of backoff. The first request or two should definitely be instant (in case there's a cache hit), but after that we should probably be polling every few seconds.

According to `./pants --explain compile`, this includes:
```

imports [ivy-imports->IvyImports_imports_ivy_imports]

Some of these are ivy-consuming tasks, but luckily they are already subject to independent ivy resolves for their classpaths.

issue_title: Jar dependencies from a jvm_tool collide with main classpath
issue_body:
If a jvm tool has transitive dependency on a jar with newer version then in 3rdparty then Ivy will resolve only the newest one.

For a real life example see description of https://rbcommons.com/s/twitter/r/2253.

issue_title: We should rename ARTIFACT to ARTIFACT_GROUP or similar
issue_body:
Here's something I've seen a couple of times now, it's not obvious that the REGISTRY_VALUE is the artifact. People think they need to create a separate ARTIFACT object to hold it. In reality you only need to do this if you are grouping artifacts into a meta artifact.

ARTIFACT_GROUP
ARTIFACT_LIST
META_ARTIFACT

would probably be more intuitive names.

```
name: ConfickerIndicators
doc: Conficker Indicators.
sources:
- type: ARTIFACT
  attributes:
    names:
      - ConfickerWindowsRegKeys
supported_os: [Windows]
urls: ['https://www.iocbucket.com/iocs/e2903921f8b21d71dc88f34bee8b2f3491ea4656']
---
name: ConfickerWindowsRegKeys
doc: Windows Conficker IOCs.
sources:
- type: REGISTRY_VALUE
  attributes:
    key_value_pairs:
    - {key: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\[RANDOM CHARACTERS]\"ImagePath"', value: '"%System%\svchost.exe -k netsvcs"'}
    - {key: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\[WORM GENERATED SERVICE NAME]\"ImagePath"', value: '%SystemRoot%\system32\svchost.exe -k netsvcs'}
    - {key: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\explorer\Advanced\Folder\Hidden\SHOWALL\"CheckedValue"', value: '0'}
    - {key: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\Tcpip\Parameters\"TcpNumConnections"', value: '00FFFFFE'}
supported_os: [Windows]
urls: ['https://www.iocbucket.com/iocs/e2903921f8b21d71dc88f34bee8b2f3491ea4656']
```

issue_title: EXIF Data Parsing Error
issue_body:
I have compared two images taken on iOS 8.1.2 and iOS 8.3.  The images taken on iOS 8.3 could not be placed into a word doc via python-docx because it was throw exception 'Unexpected EOF' in the image/helpers.py file.  I traced through the code and then looked at the images in a hex editor.

Both images and their EXIF data seem to display correctly without error in a number of image programs.

I think it's possible that in the EXIF data, (which usually has a tag_code, value_count and value_offset directory for each EXIF field), the value placed in the 'value_offset' area is the value itself if it can fit within a 4-byte field, else a relative address is placed to where that value is actually stored.  See image below for a (hopefully clear) explanation of what I found.

Thanks.
![screen-shot-2015-06-04-at-9 24 45-am](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/1898321/7985761/280db7b2-0aa1-11e5-8451-cd1fe6030be8.gif)

issue_title: snapshot code confusion over self.playlist_position
issue_body:
While reading through the snapshot code I stumbled on this:

```python
            if self.is_playing_queue and self.playlist_position > 0:
                # was playing from playlist

                if self.playlist_position is not None:
```

which I saw as a potential Python 3 bug. Since, if `self.playlist_position` can be `None`, `self.playlist_position > 0` will fail on Python 3 (unorderable types), while it was allowed under Python2.

When you then look at how the value is retrieved it goes:

```python
                position = track_info['playlist_position']
                if position != "":
                    # save as integer
                    self.playlist_position = int(position)
```

So it is indeed possible, that the property will not be changed away from init value of None.

Maybe the order of the checks in the first code snippet should be changed. This should be investigated.

issue_title: Allow users to pin a URI-M or all captures for a URI-R from their local replay UI
issue_body:
Related to #60.

When replaying a URI-M whose header and payload are accessed through another node via IPFS, the header and payload will eventually get garbage collected from the local system per https://discuss.ipfs.io/t/how-are-conflicts-handled/469/3 . Provide a UI element to allow a user to explicitly indicate that they wish to retain the URI-M (i.e., the payload and headers associated with the URI-M) on their local system. This can be accomplished by `ipfs pin`ning.

Doing so will allow a user to accumulate captures locally and will facilitate collaboration of arbitrary sets of captures.

issue_title: Pushing datalake files reads entire file into memory
issue_body:
I hit an unpleasant "bug" in Datalake today. We have a system which operates with most memory allocated to other processes during normal usage.
This system also generates some relatively large objects to push into datalake (videos, in this case).
I was notified about disk usage climbing and climbing, and upon further investigation, I found that `datalake uploader` had stopped running with a stacktrace like:
```
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/usr/local/bin/datalake", line 11, in <module>
    sys.exit(cli())
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/click/core.py", line 716, in __call__
    return self.main(*args, **kwargs)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/click/core.py", line 696, in main
    rv = self.invoke(ctx)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/click/core.py", line 1060, in invoke
    return _process_result(sub_ctx.command.invoke(sub_ctx))
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/click/core.py", line 889, in invoke
    return ctx.invoke(self.callback, **ctx.params)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/click/core.py", line 534, in invoke
    return callback(*args, **kwargs)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/datalake/scripts/cli.py", line 193, in uploader
    _uploader(**kwargs)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/datalake/scripts/cli.py", line 30, in wrapped
    return f(*args, **kwargs)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/datalake/scripts/cli.py", line 202, in _uploader
    u.listen(**kwargs)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/datalake/queue.py", line 134, in listen
    self._listen(timeout=timeout)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/datalake/queue.py", line 147, in _listen
    self._push(path)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/datalake/queue.py", line 127, in _push
    url = self._archive.push(f)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/datalake/archive.py", line 155, in push
    self._upload_file(f)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/datalake/archive.py", line 161, in _upload_file
    key.set_contents_from_string(f.read())
  File "/usr/lib/python2.7/tarfile.py", line 816, in read
    buf += self.fileobj.read()
  File "/usr/lib/python2.7/tarfile.py", line 736, in read
    return self.readnormal(size)
  File "/usr/lib/python2.7/tarfile.py", line 745, in readnormal
    return self.fileobj.read(size)
MemoryError
```

Around here: https://github.com/planetlabs/datalake/blob/d8952c71948a8d13ae5ce30322c37ae05e7bfb36/datalake/archive.py#L176 it seems that datalake is reading in the entire "content" of the file and uploading it as a binary string with the S3 API.
Is there a compelling reason to pull the entire object into memory?

If not, I would propose switching `boto.S3.Key.set_contents_from_string(f.read())` to be `boto.S3.Key.set_contents_from_file(f)` instead, as `TarFile.extractfile()` appears to cleanly support the file IO interface without reading everything in.

issue_title: JSON generation bug for neighbor-changes
Old default value for API version in documentation
issue_body:
In documentation, there is old default value for API version (`1.24`), but [in constants.py](https://github.com/docker/docker-py/blob/master/docker/client.py#L61) there is `DEFAULT_DOCKER_API_VERSION = '1.26'` (which is actually used as a default).

[documentation latest/client](http://docker-py.readthedocs.io/en/latest/client.html):
```
 from_env()
    Return a client configured from environment variables.
    The environment variables used are the same as those used by the Docker command-line client. They are:
    DOCKER_HOST
        The URL to the Docker host.
    DOCKER_TLS_VERIFY
        Verify the host against a CA certificate.
    DOCKER_CERT_PATH
        A path to a directory containing TLS certificates to use when connecting to the Docker host.
    Parameters:	
        version (str) – The version of the API to use. Set to auto to automatically detect the server’s version. Default: 1.24
```

[client.py](https://github.com/docker/docker-py/blob/master/docker/client.py#L61):
```python
 @classmethod
    def from_env(cls, **kwargs):
        """
        Return a client configured from environment variables.
        The environment variables used are the same as those used by the
        Docker command-line client. They are:
        .. envvar:: DOCKER_HOST
            The URL to the Docker host.
        .. envvar:: DOCKER_TLS_VERIFY
            Verify the host against a CA certificate.
        .. envvar:: DOCKER_CERT_PATH
            A path to a directory containing TLS certificates to use when
            connecting to the Docker host.
        Args:
            version (str): The version of the API to use. Set to ``auto`` to
                automatically detect the server's version. Default: ``1.24``
            timeout (int): Default timeout for API calls, in seconds.
            ssl_version (int): A valid `SSL version`_.
            assert_hostname (bool): Verify the hostname of the server.
            environment (dict): The environment to read environment variables
                from. Default: the value of ``os.environ``
```

issue_title: Add 'sysctl' option support
issue_body:
PR docker/docker#19265 merged 'sysctl' support into docker API as of v1.24, but there seems to be no way to use this feature with docker-py. Has anyone started work on such a feature? If not, I will create a PR for a patch that we're using internally.

The sysctl option expects a dictionary of { <name>: <value> } (for example: { "net.ipv4.ip_forward": "1" }), where the types of both objects are of type string. My patch checks if <value> is not a string type, and if that's the case, it casts it using str(). I originally intended to do the casting in docker-compose (for which I have another patch), but now that I've looked closer at the API I believe it belongs in the API. I'm open to debate on that matter though.

issue_title: Error during belief propagation with a BayesianModel (in MarkovModel.to_junction_tree).

Something even odder is that the BeliefPropagation DOES work if I use exact same model and only change the variable names! In one case I index my random variables with i, j, and k, and in the second case I use a, b, and c. I have no idea why these would behave differently. 

The error line seems to be located in MarkovModel.to_junction_tree. near line 520. 
The line that errors is 

            clique_potential *= factor_product(*clique_factors)

This happens because clique_factors is an empty list, and the subsequent reduce operation does not know what to do. 

I do not have an in depth knowledge of these algorithms, so I'm uncomfortable trying to fix it myself. 
I'm not sure if it should never be allowed to be in a state where clique_factors is empty, or if all that is needed is a check around each node's clique_potential computation that simply does not add the potential to the tree if there are no associated factors. issue_title: Do not include any obsolete properties in initial (0.9) release
issue_body:
We need to stop code-generating obsolete properties (aka attributes). When we extract the metadata that eventually gets sourced on GitHub as attributes.py, it includes some obsolete properties. We should mark them as 'codegen_method':'no'.

In a future release, when a property (or function) that was already included in the Python APIs is obsoleted, we will likely need to keep including it and handle it somehow else. A new metadata tag such as is_obsolete:True would need to be created at that point.

Deprecations and Removals

  File "C:\Python27\lib\site-packages\datapackage-0.0.1-py2.7.egg\datapackage\datapackage.py", line 327, in _load_schema
    return datapackage.schema.Schema(schema)
  File "C:\Python27\lib\site-packages\datapackage-0.0.1-py2.7.egg\datapackage\schema.py", line 35, in __init__
    self._registry = self._load_registry()
  File "C:\Python27\lib\site-packages\datapackage-0.0.1-py2.7.egg\datapackage\schema.py", line 71, in _load_registry
    return datapackage.registry.Registry()
  File "C:\Python27\lib\site-packages\datapackage-0.0.1-py2.7.egg\datapackage\registry.py", line 44, in __init__
    six.raise_from(RegistryError(e), e)
  File "C:\Python27\lib\site-packages\six-1.10.0-py2.7.egg\six.py", line 718, in raise_from
    raise value
datapackage.exceptions.RegistryError: Scheme "c" is not supported
```

Following his debugging steps, even though that exception class is `datapackage.exceptions.RegistryError`, it's actually being thrown by `tabulator`. What's happening is that at:

```python
# https://github.com/frictionlessdata/datapackage-py/blob/master/datapackage/registry.py#L108-L110
    def _get_registry(self, registry_path_or_url):
        '''dict: Return the registry as dict with profiles keyed by id.'''
        table = tabulator.topen(registry_path_or_url, with_headers=True)
        # ...
```

where `registry_path_or_url` is, by default, the path to the local `registry.csv` cache. In Linux/Mac, it'll look like `/foo/bar/baz/registry.csv`, in Windows, it'll look like `C:\foo\bar\baz\registry.csv`. That goes to `tabulator.topen`, which tries to autodetect the scheme (as none was passed). The scheme detection in [helpers.detect_scheme](https://github.com/frictionlessdata/tabulator-py/blob/master/tabulator/helpers.py) uses `urlparse(path).scheme`. Trying that method with the example paths, it returns:

```
>>> urlparse.urlparse('/foo/bar/baz/registry.csv')                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          
ParseResult(scheme='', netloc='', path='/foo/bar/baz/registry.csv', params='', query='', fragment='')
>>> urlparse.urlparse('C:\foo\bar\baz\registry.csv')                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        
ParseResult(scheme='c', netloc='', path='\x0coo\x08ar\x08az\registry.csv', params='', query='', fragment='')
```

Note that for `/foo/bar/baz/registry.csv`, the `scheme` is `''` (empty string), while for `C:\foo\bar\baz\registry.csv`, the `scheme` is `'c'`. Going back to `tabulator.topen`, the code for detecting the scheme is `scheme = helpers.detect_scheme(source) or _DEFAULT_SCHEME`. So, with a path as in Linux, it'll be `scheme = '' or _DEFAULT_SCHEME`, which ends up being `_DEFAULT_SCHEME` (file). But for a path in Windows, that line will become `scheme = 'c' or _DEFAULT_SCHEME`, so `scheme` ends up being `'c'`. As there's no scheme named `'c'`, tabulator raises an exception.

As far as I can see, that's the source of the error @jamiekt is seeing.

issue_title: Proposal: add admin-only registration mode
issue_body:
Currently registration can be either enabled or disabled.  It's just on or it's off.

There's probably a good use case for allowing registration _only_ by admins.  This would allow admins to create accounts on behalf of developers and set an initial password.  Developers can reset their password after initial login.

This helps admins avoid the "enable registration long enough for a new developer to register and then disable it again" dance.

issue_title: Symlink Support
issue_body:
Here is my proposal for how we should add Transfer's upcoming support of symlinks to the CLI:

**Task Submission** 

Add a `--recursive-symlinks [keep | copy | ignore]` option to `globus transfer` that gets passed through the SDK as the `recursive_symlinks` query parameter for Transfer.

Add a `--symlink` option to `globus transfer` with usage similar to `--recursive` on both single item and batch transfers to mark that a `transfer_symlink_item` should be used instead of a `transfer_item` to transfer a symlink rather than the target of a symlink. If both `--symlink` and `--recursive` are given for an item, the CLI should throw a usage error.

**Task Monitoring:**

Add `Symlinks` and `Recursive Symlinks` to the `TRANSFER_FIELDS` for output used by `globus task show`
that will display the new `symlinks` and `recursive_symlinks` fields in the task document.

**Symlink Creation**

Add a `globus symlink` command with usage `globus symlink [OPTIONS] ENDPOINT_ID:SOURCE_PATH TARGET_PATH`
that just passes `ENDPOINT_ID`, `SOURCE_PATH`, and `TARGET_PATH` to the SDK's new `TransferClient.operation_symlink` method.

issue_title: Enforce UUID Type on all Endpoint IDs
issue_body:
Currently, canonical names can make their way through the CLI and to the backend.

There's no need to support this into the future, so for the sake of future iterations of the CLI, we should strictly enforce that all endpoint IDs are UUIDs.

issue_title: add mean_change_quantiles
issue_body:
The idea of mean_abs_change_quantiles had been derived from discussion on the `driftbif_simulation` example and modelling the time series as Langevin equation as explained by [Siegert el al. (1998): _Analysis of data sets of stochastic systems_](http://dx.doi.org/10.1016/S0375-9601(98)00283-7). 

Therefore, not the mean absolute change per quantile, but its mean change per quantile is relevant.

`$ C:\Users\tsnyder\Miniconda3\Scripts\conda-env-script.py create tsnyder/python_training_2016_10`




    Traceback (most recent call last):
      File "C:\Users\tsnyder\Miniconda3\lib\site-packages\conda\exceptions.py", line 473, in conda_exception_handler
        return_value = func(*args, **kwargs)
      File "C:\Users\tsnyder\Miniconda3\lib\site-packages\conda_env\cli\main_create.py", line 81, in execute
        directory=os.getcwd())
      File "C:\Users\tsnyder\Miniconda3\lib\site-packages\conda_env\specs\__init__.py", line 23, in detect
        raise SpecNotFound(build_message(specs))
    conda_env.exceptions.SpecNotFound: Runtime error: Please install binstar
    Please install nbformat:
        conda install nbformat
    Conda Env Exception: environment.yml file not found
    There is no requirements.txt
```
# What to fix?
1. This shouldn't be an "unexpected" error.  If `conda env create` takes a positional parameter named `remote_definiton` and you don't have the remote client installed, that should be an expected error that has a cleaner message.   Something that says `'{} could not be resolved but you do not have the remote client installed.  Please install {}'.format(remote_definition, remote_client)` might be appropriate.
2. It shouldn't be suggesting to install `binstar` anymore.  I vaguely remember that the latest `binstar` says "Don't use me anymore" but the UX would be nicer if we didn't send people down that road.
# How did I run into this?

I'm prepping my userbase for a course that @msarahan is going to be teaching them next week.  I'll be advising them to use our Wakari Enterprise install or JupyterHub or ipython in a central environment that I have setup for the purpose of this course.  However, I do want to let people know how easy it is to get Anaconda python working on their own systems.  So, I'm putting together the instructions on how to do a clean install of miniconda + the environment they need.  Since I want to show off Anaconda Repo, I'm trying to do `conda env create` with a `remote_definition` for the first time and noticed the exception mentions installing `binstar`.

issue_title: coala-bears and conda
issue_body:
Hello, 

[coala](https://github.com/coala-analyzer/coala) is a code analysis tools that helps you fix your code and many style problems. It uses [bears](https://github.com/coala-analyzer/coala-bears) as plugins, (has over 65) to fix specific problems in many different languages.

However, these bears use external dependencies, some from ``pip``, some from ``npm``, even more. We are planning to decentralize our bears instead of holding them all together and make them ``conda`` packages, because we thought that would be awesome. However, for this to happen, we also need to make their requirements conda packages as well!

We were planning to make a script that would automatically upload commands that would install those dependencies our bears need and upload them to Anaconda using conda. But before doing this, we thought that maybe you would have something already that does something similar, or maybe you could help us a little in doing this if you're interested?

Thanks a lot for your time!

CC @sils1297, @justuswilhelm

issue_title: SSL Connection Issue
issue_body:
I just installed Curator 4.2 from the repository. It is installed on the ELK server. I created the ~/.curator/curator.yml file with the following contents:

```
---
# Remember, leave a key empty if there is no value.  None will be a string,
# not a Python "NoneType"
client:
  hosts:
    - server.domain.local (with my actual FQDN here)
  port: 5044
  url_prefix:
  use_ssl: True
  certificate: /etc/pki/tls/certs/logstash-forwarder.crt (my self signed crt file)
  client_cert:
  client_key:
  ssl_no_validate: False
  http_auth:
  timeout: 180
  master_only: False

logging:
  loglevel: INFO
  logfile:
  logformat: default
  blacklist: ['elasticsearch', 'urllib3']
```

I created an action file `delete_older_90_days.yml` with the following contents:

```
---
# Remember, leave a key empty if there is no value.  None will be a string,
# not a Python "NoneType"
#
# Also remember that all examples have 'disable_action' set to True.  If you
# want to use this action as a template, be sure to set this to False after
# copying it.
actions:
  1:
    action: delete_indices
    description: >-
      Delete indices older than 90 days (based on index name), for logstash-
      prefixed indices. Ignore the error if the filter does not result in an
      actionable list of indices (ignore_empty_list) and exit cleanly.
    options:
      ignore_empty_list: True
      timeout_override:
      continue_if_exception: False
      disable_action: False
    filters:
    - filtertype: pattern
      kind: prefix
      value: logstash-
      exclude:
    - filtertype: age
      source: name
      direction: older
      timestring: '%Y.%m.%d'
      unit: days
      unit_count: 90
      exclude:
```

When I run `curator --dry-run ~/.curator/delete_older_90_days.yml` I get the following errors:

```
2017-01-06 10:29:27,298 INFO      Preparing Action ID: 1, "delete_indices"
/opt/elasticsearch-curator/lib/python35.zip/urllib3/connection.py:337: SubjectAltNameWarning:
Certificate for garcia.magicsprings.local has no `subjectAltName`, falling back to check for a
`commonName` for now. This feature is being removed by major browsers and deprecated by RFC 2818.
(See https://github.com/shazow/urllib3/issues/497 for details.)

 - repeats that error a few times

Unable to create client connection to Elasticsearch.  Error: ConnectionError(('Connection aborted.',
RemoteDisconnected('Remote end closed connection without response',))) caused by:
ProtocolError(('Connection aborted.', RemoteDisconnected('Remote end closed connection without
response',)))
```

I tried setting `ssl_no_validate: True` but that did not resolve the issue. Then I get:

```
/opt/elasticsearch-curator/lib/python35.zip/urllib3/connectionpool.py:843: InsecureRequestWarning:
Unverified HTTPS request is being made. Adding certificate verification is strongly advised. See:
https://urllib3.readthedocs.io/en/latest/advanced-usage.html#ssl-warnings

Unable to create client connection to Elasticsearch.  Error: ConnectionError(('Connection aborted.',

issue_title: Possible 2.1.0 -> 2.2.0 regression?
issue_body:
Hello after upgrading to the newest version of elasticsearch-py, I started getting an error in my code. I stripped down the error to the following test case:

```python
from elasticsearch import Elasticsearch


apostrophe = "\xe2\x80\x98"

document = {
    "id": 1,
    "unicode_apostrophe": apostrophe.decode("utf-8"),
    "encoded_apostrophe": apostrophe}

es = Elasticsearch("http://127.0.0.1:9200")
es.index(index="test", doc_type="document", body=document, id=document["id"])
```

Running that on version 2.1.0 causes no errors and the values for `"unicode_apostrophe"` and `"encoded_apostrophe'"` show up the same in the index. Running it on 2.2.0 results in the following error:

```
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "test2.py", line 12, in <module>
    es.index(index="test", doc_type="document", body=document, id=document["id"])
  File "/home/administrator/test/venv/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/elasticsearch/client/utils.py", line 69, in _wrapped
    return func(*args, params=params, **kwargs)
  File "/home/administrator/test/venv/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/elasticsearch/client/__init__.py", line 261, in index
    _make_path(index, doc_type, id), params=params, body=body)
  File "/home/administrator/test/venv/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/elasticsearch/transport.py", line 295, in perform_request
    body = self.serializer.dumps(body)
  File "/home/administrator/test/venv/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/elasticsearch/serializer.py", line 50, in dumps
    raise SerializationError(data, e)
elasticsearch.exceptions.SerializationError: ({'encoded_apostrophe': '\xe2\x80\x98', 'id': 1, 'unicode_apostrophe': u'\u2018'}, UnicodeDecodeError('ascii', '"\xe2\x80\x98"', 1, 2, 'ordinal not in range(128)'))
```

The change that caused this seems to be in this commit: https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch-py/commit/c305dc5587963bc9e41ae8cf35bb6eff5afb199e

I'm using Python 2.7.6 (the version that comes packaged with Ubuntu 14.04).

Aside: Obviously the best thing would be to _not_ mix `str` and `unicode` in this fashion (in fact, I'm going to clean up my code to change this which will remove the error for me), but I thought that the developers might want to be aware of this.

issue_title: After migrating to 1.3.3 there is 100% CPU usage and memory leak after some time
issue_body:
## Long story short

After upgrading 1.2.0 to 1.3.3 there are CPU overusages and memory leaks on Linux (Ubuntu 16.04).
Works good on OS X 10.12.3 (Sierra) or the bug shows after too long time. I tried same script on OS X and Ubuntu and Ubuntu starts leaking after 15-30 seconds.

## Expected behaviour

No 100% CPU usage and memory leaks during whole time.

## Actual behaviour

100% CPU usage and memory leaks (even 1MB/s on many clients).

## Steps to reproduce

```python
"""Try to find leak and bugs in aiohttp which occurs on Linux."""
import asyncio
import aiohttp
import json
import urllib


def _get(loop, url, params=None):
    _connector = aiohttp.TCPConnector(loop=loop, verify_ssl=False)

    uri = 'http://consul' + url
    if params:
        uri = '%s?%s' % (uri, urllib.parse.urlencode(params))

    resp = yield from aiohttp.request(
        'GET', uri, connector=_connector, loop=loop)

    body = yield from resp.text(encoding='utf-8')

    data = json.loads(body)
    return resp.headers['X-Consul-Index'], data


@asyncio.coroutine
def _sync_service(loop, service):
    index = 0
    while True:
        try:
            index, data = yield from _get(
                loop,
                '/v1/catalog/service/{}'.format(service),
                params={'index': index}
            )
        except Exception:
            pass
        yield from asyncio.sleep(0.1)


@asyncio.coroutine
def _leak(loop, services):
    _, services = yield from _get(loop, '/v1/catalog/services')

    for i in range(50):
        for service, _ in services.items():
            loop.create_task(_sync_service(loop, service))


if __name__ == '__main__':
    services = {}
    loop = asyncio.get_event_loop()
    loop.create_task(_leak(loop, services))
    loop.run_forever()

```

## Your environment

Ubuntu 16.04

issue_title: incorrect nSL values
issue_body:
The nSL values output from `allel.nsl` disagree with `libsequence`'s version of that statistic, and also with the original code from the nSL paper.  The latter two programs completely agree.

Below is a reproducible example, using current versions of all packages available via bioconda on os x:

First, we simulate some data, analyze it using both Python tools, and write the ms block to a file so that the author's original code can be used:

```py
import msprime
# libsequence is via the pylibseq package
import libsequence.polytable
import libsequence.summstats
import libsequence.msprime
import allel
import numpy as np

print(msprime.__version__)
print(libsequence.__version__)
print(allel.__version__)

x=msprime.simulate(50,mutation_rate=250.,recombination_rate=250.,random_seed=666)

sd=libsequence.msprime.make_SimData(x)

geno_array=x.genotype_matrix()

hap_array=allel.HaplotypeArray(geno_array)

print(geno_array.shape,hap_array.n_variants,hap_array.n_haplotypes)

libseq_nsl = libsequence.summstats.nSLiHS(sd,[i for i in range(sd.numsites())])

allel_nsl = allel.nsl(hap_array)

print(len(libseq_nsl),len(allel_nsl))

with open('compare_nsl.txt','w') as f:
    for i,j in zip(libseq_nsl,allel_nsl):
        if np.isnan(i[0]) == 0:
            f.write("{} {} {}\n".format(i[0],float(i[2])/float(sd.size()),j))

# write out the msprime data in ms format so 
# that we can use the code from original
# nSL paper

with open('msblock.txt','w') as f:
    f.write('ms 50 1 -t 100\n')
    f.write('1345 124 1243\n\n')
    f.write(str(sd))
```

The results are very different.  The format is nSL_libseq DAF nSL_allel

```sh
-0.32721875992940097 0.42 0.22998406636520813
0.32721875992940097 0.58 -0.22998406636520813
0.32721875992940097 0.58 -0.22998406636520813
0.32721875992940097 0.58 -0.22998406636520813
-3.2108067608338398 0.22 1.6716065519911303
-3.2108067608338398 0.22 1.6716065519911303
-0.46427802011234487 0.38 0.4573615388228044
-0.5281175356366608 0.38 0.46307921106782235
-3.570735353281367 0.22 1.7803932063981416
-0.3678851995803196 0.42 0.7308904146638405
```

`libsequence` agrees with the results from the original nSL paper's code, where the stat is the third column in the output:

```sh
~/src/nSL/nSL -msfile msblock.txt -maxLen 50000 | head
	-> Starting nSL version:0.470000
	-> Program running with:
	 (input files)
		 -samfile = (null)
		 -hapfile = (null)
		 -adfile = (null)
		 -msfile = msblock.txt
	 (output files)
		 -txtMatrix1 = (null)
		 -txtMatrix2 = (null)
		 -txtMatrix3 = (null)
		 -maxLen = 50000
	-> Will open file:msblock.txt with mode= r
	-> According to ms command we expect
		 nChr:50	 and nRep:1
	-> number of Seqsites:4296
	-> range of positions (0,999) about: 0.00 Megabases
SNPid	SNPpos	SL	DerAllele	DAF	ANS.Length	ANS.SegSites	DER.Length	DER.SegSites
rs.ms	1	-0.327219	1	42.000000	1496	78	2687	101
rs.ms	1	0.327219	1	58.000000	2687	101	1496	78
rs.ms	1	0.327219	1	58.000000	2687	101	1496	78
rs.ms	2	0.327219	1	58.000000	2687	101	1496	78
rs.ms	2	-3.210807	1	22.000000	3279	399	2038	10
rs.ms	2	-3.210807	1	22.000000	3279	399	2038	10
rs.ms	3	-0.464278	1	38.000000	1998	136	2641	113
rs.ms	3	-0.528118	1	38.000000	1902	138	2641	113
rs.ms	4	-3.570735	1	22.000000	3125	545	2038	10
```

I tried many `-maxLen` options with the nSL program, and could never match the results from `allel`.

I used a large `maxLen` because `libsequence` assumes your data block is filtered down to the window that you want to analyze.

We're still using bin/blockstack-server and now calling the repo Blockstack Core. We should add an alias “bin/blockstack-core” to bin/blockstack-server. This way the older bin/blockstack-server will still work while we update our docs at various places.

Also, now that the CLI and Core are in the same repo, we should include Core in the installation of “blockstack” i.e.,

> pip install blockstack

will give you everything and users can then do

> $ blockstack-core start

as well as

> $ blockstack info

and there will be no need to install blockstack-core separately.

issue_body:
Would it be in the scope of this plugin to support the invert case "rerunpassed" which reruns passed tests up to N times to ensure that they are not flaky?

I understand that this would be a little "against" the name of the plugin but I assume beside another command line options almost all of the logic will be the same except the condition deciding if the test needs to be retriggered.

issue_title: Email to anonymous users cannot be saved
issue_body:
Basically `AbstractEmail.user` has `null=False` so there's no way to just simply save an email that's not hooked to a `User`.

Somewhat related, the actual email address that the email was sent to is not recorded. This could differ from a user's email because they could have changed it between when the email was generated and the present.

I'm not sure we should change the default behavior to save anonymous emails, but we should definitely allow for it to be overridden.

issue_title: Compiling ASP.NET web app with AssemblyInfo.cs in a shared project ends with a warning
issue_body:
I upgraded my local .NET Core SDK from version 2.1.201 to 2.1.300. After upgrade, the build process started reporting a warning for one of our web projects:

```
CA1016: Assemblies should be marked with AssemblyVersionAttribute
```

The setup of our solution is not entirely straight-forward in regards to `AssemblyInfo.cs` files. We have a shared project containing an `AssemblyInfo.cs` with the common properties and then each project, including the failing one, has its own.

Common file:
```
using System;
using System.Reflection;
using System.Resources;
using System.Runtime.InteropServices;

[assembly: AssemblyVersion("0.20.0.0")]
[assembly: AssemblyFileVersion("0.20.0.0")]
[assembly: AssemblyDescription("")]
[assembly: AssemblyCompany("ERNI")]
[assembly: AssemblyCopyright("...")]
[assembly: AssemblyTrademark("")]
[assembly: NeutralResourcesLanguage("en")]
[assembly: CLSCompliant(false)]
[assembly: ComVisible(false)]
```

Specific file for the failing project:
```
using System.Reflection;

[assembly: AssemblyTitle("name")]
[assembly: AssemblyProduct("name")]
```

All projects have `GenerateAssemblyInfo` set to `false`. This setup worked correctly before updating to .NET Core SDK 2.1.300. After I upgraded, the build started reporting the warning above. We usually have `TreatWarningsAsErrors` set to `true`, so the build broke on the warning.

I managed to trace the problem down to the presence of Razor views in the failing project - if I remove them, the project builds correctly. This leads me to an assumption that the new build-time compilation of views is the culprit here. Is the compiler, per chance, generating a separate assembly for the views? It looks like the compiler will not include the AssemblyInfo from the shared project in this new assembly and it will fail the analyzers in `Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.FxCopAnalyzers`.

I'm attaching a minimal repro solution that demonstrates the issue: 
[ViewCompilationIssue.zip](https://github.com/dotnet/core/files/2072199/ViewCompilationIssue.zip)

This code will fail to build due to the `TreatWarningsAsErrors`. If you exclude the Views folder from the project, it compiles.

We can work around the issue by either rewriting our assembly infos or changing the analyzer rules but this seems like a regression to me so I wanted to report it.

issue_title: Roslyn analyzers + code fixes don't work properly in VS when installed into .NET Core project via a nuget package
issue_body:
*(I can't seem to find a repo for raising VS .NET Core tooling issues, so if there's more appropriate place for this, please tell and I'll be glad to repost it there.)*

Here's what happens when a nuget package that contains Roslyn analyzers + code fixes is installed into a .NET Core project via `install-package $(AnalyzerPackage)`:

1. Analyzer package is added to `References > .NETStandard` node of Solution Explorer instead of `References > Analyzers`.
2. Which also means there's no UI for changing rule severity.
3. Analyzers are not invoked when the code is being edited. The warnings are reported only when the project is built.
4. Even then no squiggles are displayed at the warning location, the warnings are only surfaced in the error list.
5. And probably the most tragic part: the code fixes attached to the analyzer warnings simply can't be triggered.

issue_title: Validation error when importing docker definition file as hint
issue_body:
### How to reproduce:
1. Create this tool definition file, `echo.cwl`:
   
   ``` yaml
   #!/usr/bin/env cwl-runner
   
   cwlVersion: "cwl:draft-3"
   
   class: CommandLineTool
   
   hints:
     - $import: echo-docker.yml
   
   inputs:
     - id: "text"
       type: [string]
       description: |
         the text to echo
       inputBinding:
         position: 1
   
   outputs: []
   
   baseCommand:
     - echo
   ```
2. Create this docker definition file, `echo-docker.yml`:
   
   ``` yaml
   class: DockerRequirement
   dockerPull: ubuntu:16.04
   dockerFile: |
     ### Base Image
     FROM ubuntu:16.04
   ```
3. Create this job input file:
   
   ``` yaml
   text: Hello, world!
   ```
4. Execute:
   
   ``` bash
   cwl-runner --no-container echo.cwl echo_testjob.yml 
   ```

issue_title: upgrade to CKEditor v4.5.7
issue_body:
The current version of CKEditor packaged in django-ckeditor is `v4.5.3`. I am getting some buggy behavior in `v4.5.3` of CKEditor, and my problem gets solved when I switch to `v4.5.7`. I won't report the buggy behavior here, since its a problem with CKEditor, and not with django-editor directly, but if upgrading the version of CKEditor helps me solve my problem, then I am sure users of django-ckeditor can equally benefit from having the latest version. 

If you can't upgrade the library in this repo for some reason, then at the very minimum, you could list the the steps (a "recipe") to instruct users of django-ckeditor how to manually upgrade the version themselves. 

Thanks!

issue_title: Using a computed property as a relationship
issue_body:
Hello! I'm struggling to include a computed property as a relationship of my model.
I have the following models:

```
class Language(BaseModel):
    iso_code = models.CharField(max_length=2)

class Sentence(BaseModel):
    body = models.TextField()
    language = models.ForeignKey(Language)

class Dataset(BaseModel):
    name = models.CharField(max_length=100)
    description = models.TextField()
    sentences = models.ManyToManyField(Sentence, blank=True, related_name='datasets', related_query_name='dataset')
```

Now, I want my `Dataset` model to have a `Language` relationship: it should be the list of all (distinct) languages in the sentences of my dataset.

I tried to add:

```
    @property
    def get_languages(self, obj):
        return TODO
``` 

in my Dataset model definition and

```
    languages = relations.SerializerMethodResourceRelatedField(
        source='get_languages', many=True, model=Language, read_only=True)
``` 

in my DataSerializer, but to no avail (getting `AttributeError: 'property' object has no attribute 'parent'`). What is the correct way to go? Thanks!

issue_title: Change instance profile setting to roles list
issue_body:
It would be nice to be able to specify a list of roles instead of one instance profile ID. Senza should generate an instance profile on-the-fly with all roles attached. This is especially helpful as not all types of roles automatically have an instance profile ID. This way its also easier to compose different roles for the server (which will be important for getting credentials via berry).

See simplestack: https://github.com/zalando/simplestack/blob/master/resources/simplestack/template.json#L134

issue_title: Running ``c_examples`` when built with Clang with OpenMP enabled leads to segfault
issue_body:
When building the current master (3ef6d9103808c7da94f3d0e2a91a26b189d94459) with Clang 3.7.1 with OpenMP enabled running the ``c_example`` leads to a ``nullptr`` being dereferenced inside Z3.

I originally reported this in #436 however this issue seems unrelated to using the AddressSanitizer (although it does catch the segfault and reports where it happens) so I'm opening a separate issue. The issue does not appear if OpenMP support is disabled (configure with ``--noomp``). On my system it is necessary to install LLVM's OpenMP implementation (``openmp`` package on Arch Linux) for Clang to be able support OpenMP.

I built as follows.

```
CXX=clang++ CC=clang python scripts/mk_make.py --debug --build build
cd build
make -j8
make c_example
LD_LIBRARY_PATH=`pwd` ./c_example
Z3 4.4.2.0

simple_example
Segmentation fault (core dumped)
```

In gdb the backtrace is

```
#0  memory::deallocate (p=0x0) at ../src/util/memory_manager.cpp:265
#1  0x00007ffff665ee86 in buffer<unsigned int, false, 16u>::free_memory (this=0x62b660) at ../src/util/buffer.h:35
#2  0x00007ffff665ee45 in buffer<unsigned int, false, 16u>::destroy (this=0x62b660) at ../src/util/buffer.h:60
#3  0x00007ffff6652e85 in buffer<unsigned int, false, 16u>::~buffer (this=0x62b660) at ../src/util/buffer.h:95
#4  0x00007ffff680e8e5 in sbuffer<unsigned int, 16u>::~sbuffer (this=0x62b660) at ../src/util/buffer.h:254
#5  0x00007ffff7637415 in mpn_manager::mpn_sbuffer::~mpn_sbuffer (this=0x62b660) at ../src/util/mpn.h:65
#6  0x00007ffff7633fa0 in mpn_manager::~mpn_manager (this=0x62b658) at ../src/util/mpn.cpp:37
#7  0x00007ffff75ed657 in mpz_manager<false>::~mpz_manager (this=0x62b440) at ../src/util/mpz.cpp:162
#8  0x00007ffff75fa09d in mpq_manager<false>::~mpq_manager (this=0x62b440) at ../src/util/mpq.cpp:35
#9  0x00007ffff63b225b in api::context::~context (this=0x62aa78) at ../src/api/api_context.cpp:109
#10 0x00007ffff63b54a5 in dealloc<api::context> (ptr=0x62aa78) at ../src/util/memory_manager.h:94
#11 0x00007ffff63b390e in Z3_del_context (c=0x62aa78) at ../src/api/api_context.cpp:354
#12 0x0000000000405807 in simple_example () at ../examples/c/test_capi.c:704
#13 0x000000000040c188 in main () at ../examples/c/test_capi.c:2797
```

In ``memory::deallocate(void * p)`` the pointer ``p`` passed is a null.

Curiously, if I try using the Java API parse from that file (after removing the duplicated lines generated by solver.toString() and removing the (check-sat)):
```
solver.reset();
String code = FileUtils.readFileToString(new File("<path to file>.z3"), Charset.defaultCharset());
BoolExpr assertions = context.parseSMTLIB2String(code, null, null, null, null);
solver.add(assertions);
solver.check();
```
The Java API returns unsat correctly.
So, my question is, why is that after creating the assertions using the Java API and checking if satisfiable it returns unknown but using the commandline-tool/parsing it returns unsat correctly?

Thanks.

issue_title: NVARCHAR DataType max length - incorrect error message
issue_body:
Hi All.

As you know max Length for nvarchar data type is 4000. But if you enter for Length value number greater than 8000 you will get below error. 
```
DBSIZE>CREATE TABLE test_table(some_value NVARCHAR(8001));
Msg 131, Level 15, State 2, Line 1
The size (8001) given to the column 'some_value' exceeds the maximum allowed for any data type (8000).
Time: 0.054s

```
Error message says that I can create nvarchar column with max length of 8000. Let's try. 

```
DBSIZE>CREATE TABLE test_table(some_value NVARCHAR(8000));
Msg 2717, Level 16, State 2, Line 1
The size (8000) given to the parameter 'some_value' exceeds the maximum allowed (4000).
Time: 0.054s
```

Trying to create with 4000 value. 
```
DBSIZE>CREATE TABLE test_table(some_value NVARCHAR(4000));
Commands completed successfully.
Time: 0.113s
DBSIZE>
```

issue_title: Do not use a semicolon to end a multi-line query
issue_body:
Currently when editing a multiline query, we use use a semicolon+<enter> to delineate the query should be executed.

Feedback from the MVP summit: in multiline query mode, users like to enter more that one statement, which are delineated by semicolons, so using a semicolon is a bad choice. We should consider using the 'GO' keyword to execute the query, which aligns with sqlcmd.

issue_title: Add warning when using read_table on a URL from data8/ds8
issue_body:
In data-8/connector-instructors#13 Andrej ran into a cryptic error when trying to `read_table` on a `data8.berkeley.edu` link.

Since all of those links require authentication, none of them will work with `Table.read_table`. Chris suggested that we give up earlier with a more explicit message when we see one of those links. I think is a good idea as well.

issue_title: django.core.exceptions.FieldError with __date lookup and money_manager()
issue_body:
I am using Django 1.9 and django-money 0.9.1. While refining my data structure and testing some models, I noticed that the `__date` lookup that was added in Django 1.9 (https://docs.djangoproject.com/en/1.9/ref/models/querysets/#date) does not seem to work properly with `money_manager()`.

In all models which use a `MoneyField()`, `__date` lookups result in an exception like this:
`django.core.exceptions.FieldError: Cannot resolve keyword 'date' into field. Join on 'my_datetime_field' not permitted.`

When I attach an additional model manager to the model without wrapping it in `money_manager()`, the `__date` lookup works fine on that new manager.

Looking through the traceback in more detail revealed that `djmoney.models.managers.money_manager()` seems to cause the problem:
```python
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<input>", line 1, in <module>
  File "/[...]/python3.4/site-packages/django/db/models/manager.py", line 122, in manager_method
    return getattr(self.get_queryset(), name)(*args, **kwargs)

issue_title: Cannot generate a complete CFG due to the function pointer
issue_body:
when I analyze a binary file with angr, I find that I cannot get a complete CFG due to the function pointer.
The following codes are an example

.text.tcpip_init:00000278 **tcpip_init**
.text.tcpip_init:00000278
.text.tcpip_init:00000278 var_18          = -0x18
.text.tcpip_init:00000278
.text.tcpip_init:00000278                 PUSH    {R0-R2,R4,R5,LR}
.text.tcpip_init:0000027A                 MOV     R5, R0
.text.tcpip_init:0000027C                 MOV     R4, R1
.text.tcpip_init:0000027E                 BL      lwip_init
.text.tcpip_init:00000282                 LDR     R3, =tcpip_init_done
.text.tcpip_init:00000284                 LDR     R0, =mbox
.text.tcpip_init:00000286                 STR     R5, [R3]
.text.tcpip_init:00000288                 LDR     R3, =tcpip_init_done_arg
.text.tcpip_init:0000028A                 MOVS    R1, #0
.text.tcpip_init:0000028C                 STR     R4, [R3]
.text.tcpip_init:0000028E                 BL      sys_mbox_new
.text.tcpip_init:00000292                 MOVS    R3, #2
.text.tcpip_init:00000294                 STR     R3, [SP,#0x18+var_18]
.text.tcpip_init:00000296                 MOVS    R2, #0
.text.tcpip_init:00000298                 MOV.W   R3, #0x300
.text.tcpip_init:0000029C                 LDR     R1, =(**tcpip_thread**+1)
.text.tcpip_init:0000029E                 LDR     R0, =aTcpIp     ; "tcp/ip"
.text.tcpip_init:000002A0                 BL      sys_thread_new
.text.tcpip_init:000002A4                 ADD     SP, SP, #0xC
.text.tcpip_init:000002A6                 POP     {R4,R5,PC}
.text.tcpip_init:000002A6 ; ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
.text.tcpip_init:000002A8 off_2A8         DCD tcpip_init_done     ; DATA XREF: tcpip_init+Ar
.text.tcpip_init:000002AC off_2AC         DCD mbox                ; DATA XREF: tcpip_init+Cr
.text.tcpip_init:000002B0 off_2B0         DCD tcpip_init_done_arg ; DATA XREF: tcpip_init+10r
.text.tcpip_init:000002B4 off_2B4         DCD **tcpip_thread+1**      ; DATA XREF: tcpip_init+24r
.text.tcpip_init:000002B8 off_2B8         DCD aTcpIp              ; DATA XREF: tcpip_init+26r
.text.tcpip_init:000002B8 ; .text.tcpip_init ends                 ; "tcp/ip"

If I generate a CFG from "tcpip_init" function, the CFG does not include the "tcpip_thread".
This program passes the "tcpip_thread"'s address as a parameter to the "sys_thread_new" function. In fact, "tcpip_thread" is called , but angr is not recognized. I know there is no obvious call statement of the "tcpip_thrad" in the function. Is this an indirect jump?
In short, what should I do if I want to add "tcpip_thread" to the control flow graphs?

issue_title: [Question] Can you convert maFiles to the format required by this lib?
run_file模式运行report参数无效果
plt.show() 堵塞程序,  导致不能正常保存图片 plt.savefig
issue_body:
# 提 ISSUE 须知

请先阅读文档 [rqalpha文档](http://rqalpha.readthedocs.io/)

如果仍有问题的话请在 [issue列表](https://github.com/ricequant/rqalpha/issues) 中寻找是否有相关问题的解决方案

如果没有的话 麻烦开一个issue 描述以下问题：

## 1. RQAlpha的版本
latest develop branch
## 2. Python的版本
py3.6
## 3. 是Windows/Linux/MacOS or others?
Linux
## 4. 您出现问题对应的源码/或者能复现问题的简易代码 以及对应的配置

```
mod:
  sys_analyser:
    enabled: true
    # 开启 plot 功能
    plot: true
    output_file: /tmp/rq.pkl
    plot_save_file: /tmp/rq.png
    report_save_path: /tmp

```

`rqalpha run` 

先执行 [plt.show()](https://github.com/ricequant/rqalpha/blob/develop/rqalpha/mod/rqalpha_mod_sys_analyser/plot.py#L183)  后, 跳出 plt 交互图, 程序会在这里都塞. 
只有关闭 plt 交互图后, 才会继续后面的
[if savefile:](https://github.com/ricequant/rqalpha/blob/develop/rqalpha/mod/rqalpha_mod_sys_analyser/plot.py#L185)

结果是, 关闭 plt 交互图后, 不能正常保存 /tmp/rq.png. 此图片会正常生成, 但是打开时是一片空白的图片.

`rqalpha plot /tmp/rq.pkl --show --plot-save /tmp/rq.png` 会带来同样的结果.

`rqalpha plot /tmp/rq.pkl --hide --plot-save /tmp/rq.png` 不同的结果, 图片正常保存和打开.

个人建议:

[修改这里](https://github.com/ricequant/rqalpha/blob/develop/rqalpha/mod/rqalpha_mod_sys_analyser/plot.py#L182-L189)

将 `if savefile:` 放 `if show_windows:` 前面

```
    if savefile:
        fnmame = savefile
        if os.path.isdir(savefile):
            fnmame = os.path.join(savefile, "{}.png".format(summary["strategy_name"]))
        plt.savefig(fnmame, bbox_inches='tight')

    if show_windows:
        plt.show()
```

## 5. 您出现的错误堆栈日志信息

issue_title: Document how /cvmfs releases are updated
Add endpoint info in OPTIONS
issue_body:
With django rest framework, that's what we obtain with `OPTIONS`:

``` http
➜  ~ http OPTIONS https://x.x.x.x/api/fr/reports/report
HTTP/1.1 200 OK
Allow: POST, OPTIONS
Connection: close
Content-Language: fr
Content-Type: application/json
Date: Wed, 06 Jul 2016 15:01:46 GMT
Server: nginx/1.4.6 (Ubuntu)
Transfer-Encoding: chunked
Vary: Accept, Accept-Language, Cookie

{
    "actions": {
        "POST": {
            "category": {
                "label": "Catégorie", 
                "read_only": false, 
                "required": false, 
                "type": "field"
            }, 
            "comment": {
                "label": "Commentaire", 
                "read_only": false, 
                "required": false, 
                "type": "string"
            }
        }
    }, 
    "description": "Send report", 
    "name": "Report", 
    "parses": [
        "application/json", 
        "application/x-www-form-urlencoded", 
        "multipart/form-data"
    ], 
    "renders": [
        "application/json", 
        "application/json", 
        "text/html"
    ]
}
```

On Kinto (via Cornice), we have a 400 if we don't provide CORS headers:

``` http
➜  ~ http OPTIONS https://kinto.dev.mozaws.net/v1/buckets                              
HTTP/1.1 400 Bad Request
Connection: keep-alive
Content-Length: 324
Content-Type: application/json; charset=UTF-8
Date: Wed, 06 Jul 2016 15:13:05 GMT
Server: nginx

{
    "code": 400, 
    "details": [
        {
            "description": "this header is mandatory", 
            "location": "header", 
            "name": "Origin"
        }, 
        {
            "description": "this header is mandatory", 
            "location": "header", 
            "name": "Access-Control-Request-Method"
        }
    ], 
    "errno": 107, 
    "error": "Invalid parameters", 
    "message": "Access-Control-Request-Method in header: this header is mandatory"
}

```

Providing the required headers, we only receive `null`:

``` http
➜  ~ http OPTIONS https://kinto.dev.mozaws.net/v1/buckets "Origin:http://localhost" "Access-Control-Request-Method:GET"
HTTP/1.1 200 OK
Access-Control-Allow-Headers: Content-Length,Expires,Backoff,Retry-After,Last-Modified,Total-Records,ETag,Pragma,Cache-Control,Alert,Next-Page
Access-Control-Allow-Methods: GET,HEAD,POST,DELETE,OPTIONS
Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *
Access-Control-Max-Age: 3600
Connection: keep-alive
Content-Length: 4
Content-Type: application/json; charset=UTF-8
Date: Wed, 06 Jul 2016 15:16:17 GMT
Server: nginx

null
```

It would be nice if we'd mimic what DRF does!

Related: #631 swagger

/cc @almet

issue_title: Migrate scrapy to headless-chrome?
issue_body:
A few weeks ago, the chromium project announced [headless chromium](https://chromium.googlesource.com/chromium/src/+/lkgr/headless/README.md) as new, clean way to open websites in a non-UI server context.

The announcement had quite an impact in the headless-browser scene and resulted in the [resignment of the phantomJS maintainer](https://groups.google.com/forum/#!topic/phantomjs/9aI5d-LDuNE).

Since the current webkit engine of Splash dates back to 2013, I wanted to know whether there are any plans to port splash to headless chrome?

issue_title: D435 unable to stream depth and infrared simultaiouslly
Middlewares using Librealsense
issue_body:
From other posts, I am aware that librealsense is intended for direct camera access and nothing else, leaving further processing to middlewares.

Right now, whenever someone asks for this or that feature (typically, skeleton and face tracking) it is pointed to a middleware... but these links are spreaded through the entire issue list.

Given that a good chunk of developers coming here are actually looking for middlewares backed by librealsense, it would make sense (lol) to have _centralised_ middlewares section in the main page. Something like: **Middlewares using librealsense**.

issue_title: LabVIEW Wrapper HelloWorld Error
issue_body:
* Before opening a new issue, we wanted to provide you with some useful suggestions (Click "Preview" above for a better view):

    * Consider checking out SDK [examples](https://github.com/IntelRealSense/librealsense/tree/master/examples#sample-code-for-intel-realsense-cameras).
    * Have you looked in our [documentations](https://github.com/IntelRealSense/librealsense/tree/master/doc#useful-links)?
    * Is you question a [frequently asked one](https://github.com/IntelRealSense/librealsense/wiki/Troubleshooting-Q%26A)?
    * Try [searching our GitHub Issues](https://github.com/IntelRealSense/librealsense/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue) (open and closed) for a similar issue.

* All users are welcomed to report bugs, ask questions, suggest or request enhancements and generally feel free to open new issue, even if they haven't followed any of the suggestions above :)

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Required Info                         |                                                                |
|---------------------------------|------------------------------------------- |
| Camera Model                       | SR300 | 
| Firmware Version                   | 3.21.0.0 | 
| Operating System & Version | Win (10)| 
| Platform                                 | PC |
| SDK Version                            | 2.13 | 
| Language                            |  labview | 
| Segment			|  consumer   | 

### Issue Description
Goal: GreenScreen effect in Skype for Business using a new Razer Stargazer.
Status: No obvious Skype integration, 3rd party software failing, switched hats from consumer to hacker, error in Hello World.

Drivers look ok, RealSense Viewer 2.13.0 streams RGB and Coded-Light depth ok.

Running the "RealSense_HelloWorld_Depth_and_Color.vi" demo doesn't return data in either the image or intensity graph. Front panel Init errors about advanced mode look benign, no errors through config, start, capture 1, capture2 errors. "Close error" from the Front Panel:
Error calling rs2_stop (sensor:0000029BF2365E90):
 stop_streaming() failed. UVC device is not streaming!
Error calling rs2_close (sensor:0000029BF2365E90):
 close() failed. UVC device was not opened!
No Error
No Error

Highlight execution shows error cluster 1097 issues in the "rs3 start all 2.vi"  > "rs3 open multiple sensor.vi" back from a harmless looking DLL call. Might just need an DLL interface update.
Also saw a questionable Stream profile array input: two values in the array, the second value a ZERO. May indicate an upstream error I didn't probe.

<soapbox>Didn't dig past this point as I'm like 4 hours in on this off-topic :P Some new user feedback: the RealSense software stack, 3rd party app community, and documentation (even on Intel.com) is fragged, out of date, confusing and makes a high barrier to entry. Not sure how many consumers are still going at hour 4, and creating github accounts and getting involved in forums. Seems like quite a bit of frustration on forums for Razer, XSplit, and OBS.</soapbox>

issue_title: cpuid.h is missing. Compiling for ODROID XU4
issue_body:
| Required Info                         |                                                                |
|---------------------------------|------------------------------------------- |
| Camera Model                       | D435 | 
| Firmware Version                   | (Open RealSense Viewer --> Click info) | 
| Operating System & Version |   Ubuntu 16.04 | 
| Kernel Version (Linux Only)    |   4.9.38                                         | 
| Platform                                 | ODROID XU4  |
| SDK Version                            | 2.0 }                          | 


### Issue Description
I stumbled across a strange issue building librealsense 2.0 SDK.
"cpuid.h : No such file or directory." That's true, locate cpuid.h gives blank result.
I solved it the dirty way - 
I found file image.cpp that includes cpuid.h and found out there is only one bool function that uses it. And i made it always return false. That did the job for me, i rebuilt the SDK, and realsense2camera also built just fine. So I`m leaving it here in case anyone gets this issue, since I found nothing helpful on the internet. 
I also kindly suggest that you make a "light" version of the SDK, as it is clear that everything works like a charm without additional functionality. I get depth information, and RGBD just fine.

issue_title: Improving Performance of Composition Features
issue_body:
I recently compared the performance of `matminer` to `Magpie` for computing composition-based features. For ~250k entries, `matminer` requires 24 minutes to evaluate the same features that take `Magpie` 4 seconds. 

From a quick profiling run, it seems like a fair amount of the time (25%) is spent retrieving elemental properties and, [surprisingly](https://github.com/scipy/scipy/issues/3035), computing the mode of a list. Digging further, the slow part of retrieving elemental properties is sorting the properties by atomic number and calling `Composition.get_el_amt_dict()`. My plan is to adjust the AbstractData API such that it is possible to avoid performing either of these operations.

I have opened this issue to see if anyone else has ideas for speeding up the code, and to let you know I've overhauling the AbstractData API. Let me know if I should make a public branch if you want to work on this together and we can avoid merge conflicts.

issue_title: Adapt is not parsing unknown entities like number
issue_body:
Take this example.

Home Automation Intent Parser.

Automation Phrases used will be like below:

    "Mycroft Set First floor Office Temperature at Twenty"
    "Mycroft Switch On Ground floor Kitchen Table Light"
    "Mycroft Switch Off Second floor Bathroom Heater"

Automation Intent Parser:

    action_keyword = ["Set", "On", "Off"] ---> Require
    floor_keyword = ["Ground", "First", "Second"] ---> Require
    room_keyword =["Kitchen","Office","Bathroom"] ---> Require
    device_keyword =["Heater", "Cooler" "Ceiling", "Table"] ---> Require

Q:- How Intent Parser will handle "Temperature at Twenty" ?
Other two phrases (2 and 3 ) looks ok, as entities are known.
Q:- How Automation Skill will get this number Twenty in json before setting office temperature?

Twenty is not a known entity, Do we need to registered another keyword with intent parser ?
    temperature_keyword = ["one", "two"........................."fifty five"] ---> Require / Optional

If we make temperature_keyword optional and Automation Intent Parser detect no Temperature Value in phrase, then it will definitely pass null to Skill in json for temperature value.
Suppose Automation Skill put a check on Temperature value (which is null) and ask again for Temperature,
user replied "set it at Twenty",
In this case do we need another Temperature Intent Parser to parse Temperature Value?
..........................................................................................
Take another example :

A Voice Calculator

    "Calculate Five Thousand Divide by Twenty Five"

Calculator Intent Parser
    calci_keyword=["Calculate", "Calci"] ------> Require
    operation_keyword=["Multiply","Divide","Plus","Minus"] ---> Require

What about "Five Thousand" and " Twenty Five" ?
How Intent Parser will deal with Numbers ?
..........................................................................................

Stock Exchange Intent Parser

    "BUY Thousand Share of APPLE Limit two hundred point five"
    "SELL Twenty Lot of ORACLE Market"

What about "Thousand" and " two hundred point five" ?
How Intent Parser will deal with Numbers ?

issue_title: Different recording dt for diff variables
gitChangeset leaves netpyne in wrong cwd if not under git
issue_body:
If the version of NetPyNE being used is locally installed (e.g. /usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/netpyne-0.7.7-py2.7.egg/) and that dir is not under git, the gitChangeset() methods fails (which is correct behaviour).

But [this change](https://github.com/Neurosim-lab/netpyne/compare/neuroml_export...development#diff-a52fad7191c089fb6b67f5f3c6d75043L1769) leaves the current working dir in the install folder when it fails and means that methods to save the data try to write relative to that dir (and probably don't have permission).

Any reason to change from the prev func, of reverting back to currentPath even if it fails?

Explore Improving Parameter Handling
issue_body:
Currently, we have a select set of parameters, some of which overrides any setting given by the default config.yaml (and possibly passed in user's config.yaml).  I think we should explore allowing for any parameter to be passed in that can override its corresponding value in a config.yaml file.

One approach as suggested by Loren: "We can convert config.yaml to config.py so we can do more logic directly there, and we can add a function to manipulate a user-provided config.yaml and override the config dict. issue_title: Cell Comments are treated like normal text content
issue_body:
When reading a file which contains cells with comments, these comments are added to the text of the cell.
```xml
<table:table-cell table:style-name="ce227" office:value-type="string" calcext:value-type="string">
<office:annotation draw:style-name="gr2" draw:text-style-name="P2" svg:width="82.18pt" svg:height="26.62pt" svg:x="94.25pt" svg:y="0pt" draw:caption-point-x="-17.29pt" draw:caption-point-y="14.68pt"><dc:date>2017-08-25T00:00:00</dc:date>
<text:p text:style-name="P1">comment</text:p>
</office:annotation>
<text:p>key</text:p>
</table:table-cell>
```

The content of this cell after reading with pyexcel-ods will be `comment\nkey`

I think this is due to the way it is handled in `def __read_text_cell(self, cell):` [link](https://github.com/pyexcel/pyexcel-ods/blob/f5bbf7e5fd8f135b24e070e4e8e78d263cc99730/pyexcel_ods/odsr.py#L92)

It simply reads all the elements by type P, and also gets the comment-paragraph from the `<office:annotation>` element. Afterwards all paragraphs are joined together.

Would it be possible to look for P elements only as direct children of the table-cell?

issue_title: Support WSS
issue_body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
When serving visdom over HTTPS (such as with a reverse proxy), the websocket connection between the client and server continues to be insecure since the `ws` instead of `wss` protocol is used. Chrome will warn you and require you to opt-in to enable the insecure websocket connection.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
A command-line option to the visdom server that causes the served javascript to replace `ws://` with `wss://`. The Python client could use `wss` instead of `ws` if it sees that the Visdom host starts with `https` instead of `http`.

issue_title: unexpected behaviour of sampling in Pipeline
issue_body:
I have been trying to integrate the `imblearn` samplers into a pipeline. However, there is some behaviour that I do not understand. The code below is inspired by the documentation.

```python
from collections import Counter
from sklearn.datasets import make_classification
from sklearn.cross_validation import train_test_split as tts
from sklearn.neighbors import KNeighborsClassifier as KNN
from sklearn.metrics import classification_report
from imblearn.under_sampling import RandomUnderSampler
from imblearn.pipeline import Pipeline

X, y = make_classification(
    n_classes=2, class_sep=2, weights=[0.1, 0.9],
    n_informative=3, n_redundant=1, flip_y=0,
    n_features=20, n_clusters_per_class=1,
    n_samples=1000, random_state=10
)

print('Original dataset shape {}'.format(Counter(y)))

# split the dataset
X_train, X_test, y_train, y_test = tts(X, y, random_state=42)

# define estimators
rus = RandomUnderSampler(random_state=42)
knn = KNN()
```

I then use these elements to build a pipeline...

```python
# build pipeline
pipeline = Pipeline([('rus', rus), ('knn', knn)])

# fit pipline and make prediction
pipeline.fit(X_train, y_train)
y_hat = pipeline.predict(X_test)
print(classification_report(y_test, y_hat))
```

...which gives:

```
             precision    recall  f1-score   support

          0       0.80      1.00      0.89        24
          1       1.00      0.97      0.99       226

avg / total       0.98      0.98      0.98       250

```

Then I repeat same operation sequentially outside a pipeline...

```python
# sample training and test sets
X_train_sampled, y_train_sampled = rus.fit_sample(X_train, y_train)
X_test_sampled, y_test_sampled = rus.fit_sample(X_test, y_test)

# fit knn with sampled data and make prediction
knn.fit(X_train_sampled, y_train_sampled)
y_hat = knn.predict(X_test_sampled)
print(classification_report(y_test_sampled, y_hat))
```

...which gives:

issue_title: fit_sample should be named fit_transform to work in a pipeline
issue_body:
The classifiers all have fit_sample method. However to use this in sklearn pipeline they need to be called fit_transform. Then one can use all the benefits of the pipeline, especially running cross validation to include the resampling within the folds.

A workaround is to inherit from your classes and redefine it there. Note that cross validation clones the classifier so it is not possible for me to simply assign fit_transform=fit_sample.

issue_title: Inbound and tracking endpoints for Mandrill need two separate webhook keys
issue_body:
Hello!

I'm working on switching our use of djrill over to anymail with Django 1.8 on python 2.7 (we're working on upgrading to 1.11 and djrill is one of the final packages to go). We handle a lot of inbound messages so, I'm very excited to see this feature coming along :-)

Our current Mandrill setup has a single webhook which handles both tracking and inbound with a single key. As far as I can tell there is no way to have the same key between two different webhooks.

The anymail setup for the feature_inbound branch adds a second URL specifically for inbound and either tracking or inbound views will raise the following exception:

```
AnymailConfigurationError: You seem to have set Mandrill's *inbound* webhook URL to Anymail's Mandrill *tracking* webhook URL.
```

So the problem is:
* If we create a second webhook we would have a second key generated resulting in a signature error on one of the endpoints.
* If we use a single endpoint we raise the configuration error.

I'm considering adding a third signal and set of endpoints that handle any event type. I'm happy to submit a pull request for that but before I do it I want to check: is there already another solution in mind?

Thanks!
Jonathan

issue_title: Bound checks in numpy array accessors
issue_body:
The current behavior of data access methods in `py::array` and `py::array_t` is to check the dimension bounds every time the accessor is called, via the `check_dimensions` function, event when using it in a loop.

I agree that when exposing a feature to Python, it must be written in such a way that regardless of the values passed to it, it cannot crash the interpreter, but these accessors are not directly exposed to the Python users. Instead, I think that one of the main reasons people would right c++ extensions with pybind is because they want *speed*, and they might be disappointed with this behavior. https://github.com/pybind/pybind11/pull/500 already improved the situation, but as we discussed there, I would prefer to completely remove the bound checking.

issue_title: Check for PYTHONLIBS_FOUND leaves undefined variables.
issue_body:
Hi,

I noticed that due to #236, `FindPythonLibsNew.cmake` contains the following line in the beginning:

```cmake
if(PYTHONLIBS_FOUND)
    return()
endif()
```

Now, an unfortunate side effect of this, is that in such a situation the rest of the script is not run, and the variables `PYTHON_PREFIX`,  `PYTHON_MODULE_EXTENSION`, etc. are not set.

This is a problem e.g. when building [nanogui](https://github.com/wjakob/nanogui) as part of the [libigl](https://github.com/libigl/libigl) build. Indeed, because `find_package(PythonInterp REQUIRED)` is already called in the [libigl's CMake script](https://github.com/libigl/libigl/blob/c467e41fcaacd581a1120dd593b79be675d83f0e/python/CMakeLists.txt#L26), the condition above is triggered and the variables `PYTHON_PREFIX`, `PYTHON_MODULE_EXTENSION`, etc. are left undefined in the build of target `nanogui-python`.

I stumbled upon this issue because `import nanogui` doesn't work from python if the file has no extension, but it works if the target is compiled as `nanogui.so` instead.

I'm not sure what would be the workaround here, because I am not familiar with bug #236 ...
